description: Enhance review agent with build detection, bug detection, and debug code checks

userStories.description: In executor.rs, add a new async function `detect_project_and_build(cwd: &Path) -> Option<String>` that detects the project type by checking for build files (Cargo.toml, package.json, go.mod, pyproject.toml, Makefile) in the given directory, runs the appropriate build command (cargo build 2>&1, npm run build 2>&1, go build ./... 2>&1, python -m py_compile on .py files, make 2>&1), captures the combined stdout+stderr output, and returns Some(output) if the build command produced any output (especially errors/warnings), or None if no build system was detected. The function should check files in priority order and use the first match. Use tokio::process::Command for async execution. Make sure this function is NOT public - it's only used internally by the review flow.
userStories.title: Add detect_project_and_build function

userStories.description: In executor.rs, modify run_review_for_level to call detect_project_and_build(cwd) before spawning the review Claude process. If the function returns Some(build_output), include a BUILD OUTPUT section in the review prompt between the acceptance criteria and the git diff. The section should say 'BUILD OUTPUT (from running the project build command):' followed by the build output truncated to 5000 chars max. If detect_project_and_build returns None, omit the section entirely. This gives the reviewer context about build failures.
userStories.title: Integrate build output into review prompt

userStories.description: In executor.rs, update the review prompt string in run_review_for_level to add additional checks beyond acceptance criteria. The updated prompt should instruct the reviewer to ALSO check for: 1) Obvious bugs visible in the diff - undefined variables, missing imports, broken function calls, type mismatches. 2) Leftover debug code - console.log/println!/fmt.Println debugging statements that shouldn't be in production, commented-out code blocks. 3) Build errors if build output is provided. Keep the existing rules: do NOT suggest refactoring, style improvements, or architecture changes. Do NOT flag linting or formatting. Be lenient - pass if code works correctly even if not perfectly clean. Only flag things that are actually broken or clearly wrong. The review should still pass if acceptance criteria are met and no actual bugs/debug code/build errors are found.
userStories.title: Expand review prompt with code quality checks

userStories.description: Run cargo build and cargo clippy on the entire workspace. Fix any warnings (unused imports, unused variables, dead code, clippy lints) that exist in the codebase. Ensure cargo build completes with zero warnings. Check that the review prompt reads naturally and all pieces (build output section, bug checks, debug code checks, acceptance criteria) fit together coherently in the prompt string. Do NOT refactor or restructure existing code - only fix warnings and ensure clean compilation.
userStories.title: Final build verification and warning cleanup
